contract.name: v2/federal_accounts.md
contract.description: Returns a paginated list of federal accounts with managing agency metadata and budgetary_resources for a fiscal-year context (top-level response field fy; settable via filters.fy).

fy.constraints: Optional. Invalid values return 400 with a server-provided allowed-year list (observed strings '2001' through '2026').

agency_identifier.description: Filters results to federal accounts managed by a specific agency identifier.
agency_identifier.constraints: Optional. Exact string match semantics were observed: '012' returned results while '12' returned count=0 (200 with results=[]).

filters.constraints: Optional. Unknown keys inside filters are rejected with 400 (Unexpected field ... in parameter filters).

sort.description: Sort specification for the results list.

field.constraints: Optional. Invalid values return 400 with allowed values ['budgetary_resources', 'managing_agency', 'account_name', 'account_number'].

direction.constraints: Optional. Observed accepted values include 'asc' and 'desc'.

sort.constraints: Optional. Unknown keys inside sort are rejected with 400 (Unexpected field ... in parameter sort). Observed defaults: omitting sort sorts by budgetary_resources ascending; providing sort.field without direction defaults direction to asc (observed for field=budgetary_resources); providing sort.direction without field defaults field to budgetary_resources.

limit.constraints: Optional. Default is 10 when omitted. Min 1 (limit=0 -> 422). Max 100 (limit=101 -> 422). Digit-only strings (e.g., '5') are accepted; non-integer numerics (e.g., 2.5) return 400.

page.constraints: Optional. Default is 1 when omitted. Min 1 (page=0 -> 422). Digit-only strings (e.g., '2') are accepted; non-integer numerics (e.g., 2.5) return 400. Pages beyond the last return 200 with results=[].

keyword.constraints: Optional. When omitted, response keyword is null. Empty string '' is rejected with 422. Whitespace-only strings are accepted but echoed as keyword='' and behave like an unfiltered search (count unchanged).

count.description: Total number of federal accounts matching the request.

limit.description: Page size used for this response.

keyword.description: Echo of keyword input: null when omitted; otherwise a string (can be empty string when whitespace-only keyword is provided).

next.description: Next page number when hasNext=true, otherwise null.

previous.description: Previous page number when hasPrevious=true, otherwise null.

hasNext.description: Indicates whether another page is available after the current one.

hasPrevious.description: Indicates whether a page exists before the current one.

results.description: Array of federal account records (can be empty).

account_id.description: Unique identifier for the federal account.

agency_identifier.description: Managing agency identifier code as text.

managing_agency_acronym.description: Managing agency acronym; null is possible (observed).

budgetary_resources.description: Budgetary resources for the requested FY; can be null, zero, or negative.

results.account_name: Universal Service Fund, Federal Communications Commission
Salaries and Expenses, Commission on Combating Synthetic Opioid Trafficking
results.managing_agency: Commission On Combating Synthetic Opioid Trafficking

quirks: Default limit is 10 when omitted (documentation says 50).
Maximum supported limit is 100; higher values are rejected with 422.
Omitting sort sorts by budgetary_resources ascending (docs claim sort.direction default desc).
Response always includes top-level keyword: null when omitted; otherwise a string (including empty string when whitespace-only keyword is provided).
filters.fy rejects out-of-range values with 400 and includes the allowed FY strings in the detail message (observed list '2001'–'2026').
Pages beyond the last page return 200 with results=[] (not an error).
Digit-only strings for page/limit are accepted and coerced to integers; floats/decimals are rejected with 400.
managing_agency_acronym can be null in results.
Error responses (400/405/422) are JSON objects with a top-level detail string.
risks: Clients must handle 422 (bounds) and 400/405 (type/enum/parse/method) errors; error payloads are JSON with a top-level detail string.
Default limit=10 and max limit=100 require pagination for full enumeration; clients expecting limit=50 will under-fetch unless they set limit explicitly.
Pages beyond the last return 200 with results=[], so callers should stop paginating based on hasNext/next/results length rather than expecting an error.
budgetary_resources can be null, zero, or negative; consumers must not assume a non-null positive amount.
managing_agency_acronym can be null even when other fields are present; consumers must not assume it is always a string.
filters.agency_identifier is an exact string match; unpadded inputs (e.g., '12' vs '012') returned count=0 with 200, which can mask user input errors.
filters.fy only accepts server-validated fiscal year strings; hard-coding an out-of-range FY will fail with 400 (with an allowed-year list in detail).
Whitespace-only keyword inputs are accepted and behave like unfiltered searches while echoing keyword=''; UIs should trim/validate before sending.
Implement retries/backoff for network-level failures: probes recorded status=0 connection closes without an HTTP response.
gaps: Docs indicate account_name/account_number may be null; nulls were not observed in sampled probes, so nullability remains possible.
Exact keyword search field coverage beyond observed account_name/account_number matches is not exhaustively verified.
Intermittent connection drops (TLS close with no HTTP status) were observed during discovery; trigger conditions remain unclear.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"count":2253,"limit":10,"page":1,"fy":"2026","keyword":null,"next":2,"previous":null,"hasNext":true,"hasPrevious":false,"results":[{"agency_identifier":"027","account_id":4595,"account_name":"Universal Service Fund, Federal Communications Commission","account_number":"027-5183","budgetary_resources":-18447414770.81,"managing_agency":"Federal Communications Commission","managing_agency_acronym":"FCC"},{"agency_identifier":"011","account_id":3355,"account_name":"Contribution to the North American Development Bank, Funds Appropriated to the President","account_number":"011-1008","budgetary_resou

probes.notes: Confirm default behavior: empty JSON body returns 200 with default limit/page/fy and sorted results.
Confirm filters.fy works: sets response fy and budgetary_resources values while leaving count unchanged.
Error surface: filters.fy rejects values outside the server-defined certified year list (expect 400 with allowed years).
Confirm filters.agency_identifier narrows results (expect count < unfiltered).
Empty dataset representation: unpadded agency_identifier can yield 200 with count=0 and results=[].
Error surface: unexpected fields inside filters are rejected (expect 400 Unexpected field).
Error surface: invalid sort.field should return 400 with allowed field list.
Error surface: extra keys inside sort are rejected (expect 400 Unexpected field).
Confirm keyword search works and echoes keyword in response; should reduce count vs unfiltered.
Null/empty distinction: whitespace-only keyword is accepted, is trimmed to empty string in response, and behaves like unfiltered search.
Validate keyword min-length: empty string should be rejected (expect 422 below-min).

probes.notes: Nullability probe: managing_agency_acronym can be null for some federal accounts.

Method surface: GET is not allowed; endpoint is POST-only (expect 405 with JSON detail).

probes.notes: Error surface: malformed JSON body returns 400 with parse error detail.
fail - remote server closed TLS connection with no HTTP status when filters.fy was supplied

mismatches: Documentation lists default limit as 50, but live responses default to 10.
Documentation claims sort.direction defaults to 'desc', but omitting direction defaults to 'asc' (and omitting sort entirely sorts by budgetary_resources asc).
Documentation omits the keyword field in the 200 response payload, but it is always present (null when keyword is omitted).
Documentation does not describe an upper bound for limit, but the API enforces limit <= 100 (422 when exceeded).
Documentation describes filters.fy as a string, but the API enforces an enumerated list of certified fiscal years (400 when outside the allowed set).
Documentation marks managing_agency_acronym as required string, but live data includes null values.
gaps: Docs indicate account_name/account_number may be null; nulls were not observed in sampled probes, so nullability remains possible.
Exact keyword search field coverage beyond observed account_name/account_number matches is not exhaustively verified.
Intermittent connection drops (TLS close with no HTTP status) were observed during discovery; trigger conditions remain unclear.
risks: Clients must handle 422 (bounds) and 400/405 (type/enum/parse/method) errors; error payloads are JSON with a top-level detail string.
Default limit=10 and max limit=100 require pagination for full enumeration; clients expecting limit=50 will under-fetch unless they set limit explicitly.
Pages beyond the last return 200 with results=[], so callers should stop paginating based on hasNext/next/results length rather than expecting an error.
budgetary_resources can be null, zero, or negative; consumers must not assume a non-null positive amount.
managing_agency_acronym can be null even when other fields are present; consumers must not assume it is always a string.
filters.agency_identifier is an exact string match; unpadded inputs (e.g., '12' vs '012') returned count=0 with 200, which can mask user input errors.
filters.fy only accepts server-validated fiscal year strings; hard-coding an out-of-range FY will fail with 400 (with an allowed-year list in detail).
Whitespace-only keyword inputs are accepted and behave like unfiltered searches while echoing keyword=''; UIs should trim/validate before sending.
Implement retries/backoff for network-level failures: probes recorded status=0 connection closes without an HTTP response.
